scenarios.title: Volatility clustering keeps tomorrow's forecast persistently elevated and typed forecast

gherkin.given: An asset had a violent move today and its estimated persistence (alpha+beta or lambda) is high
gherkin.when: RLVOL produces the one-day-ahead forecast
gherkin.then: Tomorrow's forecast volatility is elevated relative to the long-run level and the value is typed forecast, never an observed fact

linkedTests: scripts/selftest.mjs#RLVOL EWMA and GARCH forecasts keep high persistence elevated above the long-run and stay typed forecast
tests/volatility-sizing-lab.spec.mjs#Regression BS-001: high-persistence forecast stays elevated and typed forecast

scenarios.title: The storm-gauge regime percentile always renders its trailing window and observation count

gherkin.given: The current forecast volatility is at a high percentile
gherkin.when: The storm-gauge regime is rendered in Simple or Power
gherkin.then: The percentile displays its trailing window and observation count and is never presented as an absolute cross-asset-comparable danger score

linkedTests: scripts/selftest.mjs#RLVOL volPercentile always returns its trailing windowRef and regimeBand maps thresholds
tests/volatility-sizing-lab.spec.mjs#Regression BS-002: storm-gauge percentile always renders its trailing window and observation count

scenarios.title: The sizing multiplier throttles in a storm with a worked cash example

gherkin.given: A user target annualized volatility of 15% and a forecast annualized volatility of 30%
gherkin.when: RLVOL computes the sizing multiplier
gherkin.then: The multiplier is approximately 0.5, a worked cash example on the user notional is present, and the read states it applies only if a separate signal fires

linkedTests: scripts/selftest.mjs#RLVOL sizing multiplier is min(cap, targetVol over max(floor, forecastVol)) with a worked example
tests/volatility-sizing-lab.spec.mjs#Regression BS-003: sizing multiplier throttles to about half in a storm with a worked example

scenarios.title: A near-zero forecast cannot explode the sizing multiplier

gherkin.given: A forecast annualized volatility approaching zero, for example a halted or pegged asset
gherkin.when: RLVOL computes the sizing multiplier
gherkin.then: A floor on forecast volatility bounds the multiplier so it hits the cap and never diverges toward infinity

linkedTests: scripts/selftest.mjs#RLVOL near-zero forecast vol floors the multiplier at the cap and never diverges
tests/volatility-sizing-lab.spec.mjs#Regression BS-004: near-zero forecast vol floors the multiplier at the cap

scenarios.title: No directional claim is ever emitted in Simple or Power

gherkin.given: Any asset, regime, forecast, or sizing output
gherkin.when: The tool renders Simple or Power view
gherkin.then: No panel, label, badge, axis, marker, or summary implies price direction, a target, a top, or a bottom

linkedTests: tests/volatility-sizing-lab.spec.mjs#Regression BS-005: no directional element appears in Simple or Power

scenarios.title: A GARCH fit is labeled a lightweight optimizer, never institutional MLE

gherkin.given: The user enables the optional GARCH(1,1) fit
gherkin.when: The fitted parameters are displayed
gherkin.then: The method is labeled a lightweight in-browser optimizer and is never presented as institutional maximum-likelihood estimation

linkedTests: scripts/selftest.mjs#RLVOL GARCH fit is a labeled lightweight optimizer and never institutional MLE
tests/volatility-sizing-lab.spec.mjs#Regression BS-006: GARCH fit is labeled a lightweight optimizer not MLE

scenarios.title: The backtest question is a link-out, not an in-tool result

gherkin.given: The user wants to know whether vol-targeting makes money on the asset
gherkin.when: The user requests a backtest
gherkin.then: The tool deep-links to strategy-validation-lab with context and displays no in-tool single-path performance verdict

linkedTests: scripts/selftest.mjs#RLVOL buildBacktestDeepLink emits an allowlisted strategy-validation-lab URL and no verdict
tests/volatility-sizing-lab.spec.mjs#Regression BS-007: backtest is a deep-link with no in-tool verdict

scenarios.title: Managed-market low volatility is marked, not treated as safe

gherkin.given: An asset whose history indicates a peg, band, or halt regime
gherkin.when: The regime is computed and rendered
gherkin.then: The read is marked managed-suppressed and low realized volatility is never presented as automatically full-size or safe

linkedTests: scripts/selftest.mjs#RLVOL detectManagedSuppression flags peg band or halt low volatility as managed-suppressed
tests/volatility-sizing-lab.spec.mjs#Regression BS-008: managed-suppressed history is marked, not calm/full-size

scenarios.title: Insufficient history yields an explicit unavailable state with exact counts

gherkin.given: An asset with fewer valid returns than the declared minimum
gherkin.when: The forecast, regime, or sizing read is requested
gherkin.then: The read is Unavailable with exact required-versus-available observation counts and never defaults to zero, neutral, calm, or full size

linkedTests: scripts/selftest.mjs#RLVOL below-minimum coverage is INSUFFICIENT_HISTORY with exact required-versus-available counts
tests/volatility-sizing-lab.spec.mjs#Regression BS-009: insufficient history is unavailable with exact counts

scenarios.title: Simple and Power cannot disagree for one asset, window, and controls

gherkin.given: The same asset, window, and controls
gherkin.when: Both Simple and Power views render
gherkin.then: They consume one decisionId and never show a different regime, forecast, persistence, or multiplier

linkedTests: scripts/selftest.mjs#RLVOL one decision identity feeds Simple Power owner-read and deep-link projections
tests/volatility-sizing-lab.spec.mjs#Regression BS-010: Simple and Power share one decision identity

scenarios.title: A non-convergent GARCH falls back to the labeled EWMA closed form

gherkin.given: The optional GARCH optimizer fails stationarity or convergence
gherkin.when: The model is resolved
gherkin.then: The tool falls back to the EWMA closed form and labels the fallback rather than showing a broken or silent result

linkedTests: scripts/selftest.mjs#RLVOL non-convergent GARCH resolves to the labeled EWMA closed-form fallback
tests/volatility-sizing-lab.spec.mjs#Regression BS-011: non-convergent GARCH falls back to labeled EWMA

scenarios.title: EWMA and GARCH disagreement is shown as an evidence conflict, not averaged

gherkin.given: The EWMA prior and a GARCH fit imply materially different persistence
gherkin.when: Persistence is rendered
gherkin.then: Both are shown as an explicit evidence conflict and are never silently averaged into one number

linkedTests: scripts/selftest.mjs#RLVOL material EWMA-vs-GARCH persistence divergence opens an evidence conflict and is never averaged
tests/volatility-sizing-lab.spec.mjs#Regression BS-012: EWMA-vs-GARCH persistence divergence is shown not averaged

scenarios.title: A realized estimate is never relabeled a forecast

gherkin.given: A trailing realized-volatility read over a rolling window
gherkin.when: It is displayed or published to the owner read
gherkin.then: It is typed realized and never presented as a forward forecast

linkedTests: scripts/selftest.mjs#RLVOL realized reads are typed realized and never relabeled forecast in the owner read
tests/volatility-sizing-lab.spec.mjs#Regression BS-013: realized is never relabeled a forecast in the owner read

scenarios.title: Long history is best-effort and never headlines a multi-decade claim

gherkin.given: The user requests more than the default 5-year history reach
gherkin.when: Longer bars are fetched best-effort
gherkin.then: Any multi-decade coverage is caveated as best-effort and no single-path multi-decade outperformance number is reproduced as evidence of edge

linkedTests: scripts/selftest.mjs#RLVOL longer history is best-effort caveated and projects no multi-decade single-path number
tests/volatility-sizing-lab.spec.mjs#Regression BS-014: longer history is caveated and reproduces no multi-decade claim

scenarios.title: The volatility tool is registered identically across the route registry trio

gherkin.given: The volatility-sizing-lab route and its bounded universe
gherkin.when: The registry is validated across tools.json, index.html, and rlnav.js
gherkin.then: Identity, order, label, icon, and file basename match across all three surfaces and the universe validates as a closed contract

linkedTests: scripts/selftest.mjs#tool registry parity: volatility-sizing-lab is registered identically across tools.json, index.html, and rlnav.js
scripts/selftest.mjs#RLVOL validateUniverse accepts the closed volatility-sizing universe and rejects unknown keys

scenarios.title: Cache-first partial paint renders synchronous non-blank canvases with a text and table fallback

gherkin.given: The same-origin cache holds a mixture of fresh, stale, short, and missing bars
gherkin.when: The production volatility page opens
gherkin.then: The Simple structure paints from valid cache before any network completion, Power canvases draw synchronously without requestAnimationFrame, and each canvas has an adjacent summary and same-data table

linkedTests: tests/volatility-sizing-lab.spec.mjs#Cache-first partial paint renders synchronous non-blank canvases with text and table fallback

scenarios.title: Controls recompute one decision without any market-data request

gherkin.given: The production page has loaded its bar snapshot
gherkin.when: The user changes asset, mode, estimator, term length, target vol, or notional
gherkin.then: One local computation updates Simple, Power, accessible summaries, and the owner read while zero market-data requests are caused by the control change

linkedTests: tests/volatility-sizing-lab.spec.mjs#Controls recompute one decision without any market-data request

scenarios.title: Power canvases carry aria-labels and same-data tables on desktop and mobile

gherkin.given: The Power view at 1440 and 390 CSS-pixel widths
gherkin.when: The forecast term, persistence, and estimator-comparison canvases render
gherkin.then: Each canvas has an aria-label, an adjacent current-value summary, and a same-data table, and the layout shows no clipped controls or horizontal page overflow

linkedTests: tests/volatility-sizing-lab.spec.mjs#Power canvases carry aria-label and same-data table on desktop and mobile

scenarios.title: Market Brief surfaces the volatility regime shift from the owner read without recomputing

gherkin.given: The registry includes a current normalized volatility owner read or an explicit unavailable outcome
gherkin.when: Market Brief renders the volatility item
gherkin.then: It surfaces the window-visible regime and sizing throttle with the owner as-of and deep link, and a stale or unavailable owner read renders a named coverage outcome rather than an invented synthesis

linkedTests: scripts/validate-brief-payload.mjs#Market Brief payload includes the registered volatility-sizing-lab owner read
tests/volatility-sizing-lab.spec.mjs#Registered Volatility Sizing tool publishes one owner read and Market Brief renders it without recompute

scenarios.title: Deterministic browser and Node parity with CommonJS purity for one decisionTime

gherkin.given: A pre-existing globalThis.RLVOL sentinel and one complete decision input including an explicit decisionTime
gherkin.when: rlvol.js is imported through CommonJS and buildVolDecisionRead runs twice on the same input
gherkin.then: The sentinel global is unchanged, computedAt equals the input decisionTime, and both runs produce byte-identical canonical output and one deterministic decisionId

linkedTests: scripts/selftest.mjs#RLVOL CommonJS import preserves the existing global and explicit decisionTime is deterministic
./rlvol.js#RLVOL CommonJS import preserves the existing global and explicit decisionTime is deterministic

scenarios.title: The owner read projects summary values with no raw bars or restricted payload

gherkin.given: A ready volatility decision
gherkin.when: projectVolToolRead builds the versioned rl-tool-read/v1 owner read
gherkin.then: The read carries only summary metrics with the window-visible percentile and typed forecast and realized values, and contains no raw bars, no restricted payload, and no source URL beyond the tool deep-link

linkedTests: scripts/selftest.mjs#RLVOL projectVolToolRead emits summary-only owner read with no raw bars or restricted payload
tests/volatility-sizing-lab.spec.mjs#Registered Volatility Sizing tool publishes one owner read and Market Brief renders it without recompute
